01 / Work

Software

A running list of things I have built: open-source tools, native apps, and small utilities that make a day a little better. It grows as I ship.

PythonpytestCI
★ 838

diff_cover

Finds the lines a diff changed that still lack test coverage, so review and CI can focus on what is new instead of the whole codebase. It has become a common fixture in Python CI pipelines, and I have put a lot of work into it over the years as its maintainer.

Originally created by Will Daly (diff-cover) and Sarina Canelake (diff-quality) with support from edX. I maintain it today.

Repo →
PythonwebIoT

HowHotIsTheShop

A small web page that reports the live temperature inside my wife's woodshop, so she can tell whether the glue will set and the finish will cure before she walks out there. There was no public API for the sensor, so I snooped the official app's calls and reverse engineered one. Tiny, sturdy, and used most days.

Live → Repo →
SwiftmacOSBluetoothreverse-engineering

SmartControl

A native macOS menu bar app for my Sennheiser MOMENTUM 4 headphones: battery, noise control, equalizer, and multipoint, with no Electron in sight. Building it meant reverse engineering the headphones' GAIA Bluetooth protocol over RFCOMM to add multipoint, the equalizer commands, and live push updates.

Built on zaval/sennheiser-desktop-client, which first reverse engineered the GAIA v3 command schema.

Repo →
SwiftmacOSSwiftUI

hueControl

A macOS menu bar app for Philips Hue lights, written in SwiftUI. Flip rooms on and off, jump between scenes, and tune color and brightness from the menu bar without opening the official app.

Repo →
KotlinAndroidJetpack Compose
Google Play

Scoundroid

An Android build of Scoundrel, the single-deck rogue-like card game, written in Kotlin and Jetpack Compose and shipped on the Google Play Store. Original game design by Zach Gage and Kurt Bieg.

Google Play → Repo →
TypeScriptcivic data
Retired

MACourtReporter

Tracked and reported on the activities of the Massachusetts legislature. Retired now, but a fun experiment in turning dry civic data into something a person would actually read.

Repo →
+
